title: Starter Installation Guide #131
summary: A new vehicle harness end is already installed on your replacement starter.

steps.title: Disconnect battery
steps.instruction: Remove the negative battery cable first. Wait 5 minutes for capacitors to discharge if the vehicle has airbags.

steps.title: Compare old vs new
steps.instruction: Lay both starters side by side. Verify pinion gear tooth count, drive direction, mounting bolt pattern, and solenoid terminal positions match.
steps.warning: A new starter that 'looks different' may still be correct — match function not appearance.

steps.title: Remove electrical connections
steps.instruction: Mark and remove the B+ cable (large terminal) and S wire (small terminal). Photograph the routing before disconnecting.

steps.title: Remove mounting bolts
steps.instruction: Support the starter as you remove the final bolt — most weigh 8–15 lbs and will fall.

steps.title: Inspect the flywheel ring gear
steps.instruction: Rotate the engine by hand and check every tooth visible through the starter opening. Damaged teeth will destroy the new starter within minutes.
steps.spec: No chipped, rounded, or missing teeth

steps.title: Clean the mounting surface
steps.instruction: Wire-brush the engine block where the starter mounts. The starter grounds through this surface — corrosion here causes slow cranking.

steps.title: Test-fit the new starter
steps.instruction: Verify the pinion gear-to-flywheel clearance with a 1/8" drill bit shank between a flywheel tooth and the pinion. Add or remove shims as needed.

steps.title: Install and torque mounting bolts
steps.instruction: Snug all bolts before final torque. Torque in a cross-pattern if there are 3+ bolts.

steps.title: Reconnect electrical
steps.instruction: B+ cable first, then S wire. Apply dielectric grease to terminals. Torque the B+ nut to spec — overtightening cracks the solenoid housing.

steps.title: Reconnect battery and test
steps.instruction: Negative cable last. Crank the engine — it should start within 2 seconds with no abnormal sound.

warnings: Disconnect the negative battery cable before any work — accidental contact between the B+ cable and ground will weld tools and start fires.
Never strike a modern PMGR (permanent magnet gear reduction) starter with a hammer. The ceramic magnets inside will shatter and the starter will fail within minutes.
Do not crank the engine for more than 15 seconds continuously — starter windings overheat quickly. Wait 2 minutes between attempts.
tips: If the new starter sounds 'different' from the old one, it's normal. PMGR starters spin faster and sound higher-pitched than older direct-drive units.
Save the old starter core — most parts stores charge a $20–50 core deposit that's refunded on return.
On Nissan vehicles, intermittent no-crank is more often the BCM or relay than the starter itself. Test both before condemning the starter.

title: General Procedure Diagnostic Guide #28
summary: System operating outside expected parameters
causes: Sensor or actuator out of specification

tools.name: OBD-II scan tool with bidirectional control

warnings: Wear appropriate PPE — safety glasses minimum, gloves for chemical exposure.
Never work under a vehicle supported only by a jack. Use rated jack stands on factory lift points.
tips: When the symptom doesn't match your diagnosis, the diagnosis is wrong — not the symptom. Re-test before replacing parts.
Always check for TSBs before replacing expensive components. The fix is often a reflash or revised part.

title: General Procedure Installation Guide #3
summary: Procedures vary significantly between vehicle platforms

steps.title: Reference the service manual
steps.instruction: Procedures vary significantly between vehicle platforms. Always reference the factory service manual for your specific vehicle.

steps.title: Disconnect the battery
steps.instruction: Negative cable first. Wait 5 minutes for capacitors to discharge before working on electrical components.

steps.title: Document existing configuration
steps.instruction: Photograph wiring routing, hose connections, and bracket arrangements before disassembly.

steps.title: Perform the procedure per service manual
steps.instruction: Follow the manufacturer sequence exactly — many procedures have steps that are non-obvious but critical.

steps.title: Verify torque specifications
steps.instruction: Confirm every fastener torque against the service manual. Generic 'feels tight' values cause comebacks.

steps.title: Test before declaring complete
steps.instruction: Verify the original symptom is resolved AND no new issues have been introduced.

warnings: Wear appropriate PPE — safety glasses minimum, gloves for chemical exposure.
Never work under a vehicle supported only by a jack. Use rated jack stands on factory lift points.
tips: When the symptom doesn't match your diagnosis, the diagnosis is wrong — not the symptom. Re-test before replacing parts.
